column_description: the cholesterol content in the recipe
the sodium content in the recipe
value_description:
commonsense evidence:
Salt/Sodium-Free - Less than 5 mg of sodium per serving
Very Low Sodium - 35 mg of sodium or less per serving
Low Sodium -140 mg of sodium or less per serving
Reduced Sodium - At least 25% less sodium than the regular product
Light in Sodium or Lightly Salted - At least 50% less sodium than the regular product
No-Salt-Added or Unsalted - No salt is added during processing - but these products may not be salt/sodium-free unless stated

column_description: the iron content in the recipe
value_description:
commonsense evidence: if iron > 20mg, it will lead to  
 constipation 
 feeling sick 
 being sick 
 stomach pain 
question could mention any of functions listed before.

column_description: the vitamin c content in the recipe
value_description:
commonsense evidence: Vitamin C, also known as ascorbic acid, if the VC is higher, it will:
 helping to protect cells and keeping them healthy 
 maintaining healthy skin, blood vessels, bones and cartilage 
 helping with wound healing 
question could mention any of functions listed before.

column_description: the vitamin a content in the recipe
value_description:
commonsense evidence:
higher --> beneficial to 
 helping your body's natural defense against illness and infection (the immune system) work properly 
 helping vision in dim light 
 keeping skin and the lining of some parts of the body, such as the nose, healthy 
question could mention any of functions listed before.
